input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension on any property:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Continue"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework automatically re-evaluates these bindings when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// Extension method for Color properties
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

// Generic version for any property type
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Trigger | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` changes automatically |
| Scope | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| Resource alternative | You can also define `Light`/`Dark` `AppThemeBinding` in a `ResourceDictionary` for reuse |
| Detect manually | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified` |

This requires no platform-specific code — MAUI handles the OS theme detection on iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS.

This is a knowledge/explanation question rather than a code modification task on an existing file. I'll provide a concise but complete example showing both XAML and C# approaches.
